title: update mirror to upstream main + verify #373 fix lands in fork
description:
The agent-orchestrator-mirror at ~/projects_reference/agent-orchestrator-mirror is behind upstream/main.

ACTIONS:
1. Reset mirror main to upstream/main (a37a2109)
2. Verify the #373 send-message-retry branch changes are included (or cherry-pick if not yet merged upstream)
3. Sync fork (jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator) with the upstream tmux.ts and runtime-tmux changes

CONTEXT: Upstream fix handles Enter-timing for long pastes. Our bd-tln handles dead-agent-CLI detection. Both are needed — they fix different failure modes of the same symptom (idle workers after ao send).
notes: Roadmap index: roadmap/next-priority-fixes.md · epic bd-8wm. Coordinate with bd-qhf (Enter timing) and bd-tln (dead CLI).

title: fix(session-manager): rmSync fallback orphans .git/worktrees entries
description: In pruneStaleWorktrees, when git worktree remove fails, the rmSync fallback deletes the directory but leaves stale .git/worktrees/ entries. Fix: also clean up the specific .git/worktrees/<name> entry after rmSync.

title: Evidence bundle generation and review gate
description: Two-stage evidence review: (1) generate bundles at docs/evidence/ with CI check runs, file diffs, changed files, verdict.json. (2) LLM review of bundles returns PASS/FAIL for merge gate condition 6. Ref: jleechanclaw evidence_bundle.py + evidence_review_gate.py

title: harness: add namespace alignment check to /auton — compare lifecycle-worker CWD against canonical ~/.openclaw, flag mismatch as critical
Config flag to skip getAutomatedComments polling
description: Add config option (scm.skipAutomatedCommentPolling) to disable getAutomatedComments in maybeDispatchReviewBacklog for repos without Bugbot. Saves 1+ REST calls per session per cycle. Parent: bd-8y9

title: fix(review-backlog): pollCounters Map never cleaned in production — bounded memory leak
description: The pollCounters Map in review-backlog.ts is module-level and persists across poll cycles. resetAllReviewBacklogCounters() only called in tests. Sessions deleted externally leave entries forever. Fix: prune entries for session IDs not in current session list at end of each poll cycle.

title: feat: ao-gc.sh — periodic stale worktree + tmux GC script
fix(lifecycle): reactions fire on dead sessions before cleanup — stale ao send to killed workers
description: When a tmux session is killed manually, the AO session metadata persists. The lifecycle-worker keeps polling it, detecting PR state (e.g. CHANGES_REQUESTED), and firing reactions (ao send) to a dead session + notifying OpenClaw. Root cause: reaction evaluation runs before agentDead cleanup. Fix: check session liveness BEFORE evaluating reactions, or clean up metadata immediately on tmux death detection.

title: fix(lifecycle): auto-kill on transient SCM failures destroys worktrees (PR #127)
description: When agentDead=true and SCM check throws (network error, rate limit), the catch block falls through and returns killed, triggering worktree destruction. A persistently dead agent with persistent SCM failures becomes a zombie, or worse, gets its worktree deleted on a transient blip. Fix: add consecutive-failure counter before returning killed, or restrict auto-kill to merged status only.

title: CRITICAL: pruneStaleWorktrees tmux name mismatch deletes ALL active worktrees
description: In session-manager.ts:1679, tmuxName = entry.name (e.g. 'ao-748') but real tmux sessions are '{hash}-ao-748'. has-session -t ao-748 always fails, so ALL AO worktrees are treated as stale and deleted. Fix: prefix with config hash, or scan tmux list-sessions for suffix match.

title: Lifecycle-worker cannot discover/respawn sessions for PRs with unresolved CR feedback
description:
## Problem

Two related gaps in the lifecycle-worker autonomous loop:

### 1. Split data namespace — sessions invisible across config paths
Sessions created by the ao-orchestrator (running from ~/projects_reference/agent-orchestrator/) use a different config path than the lifecycle-worker (using ~/.openclaw/agent-orchestrator.yaml). Each config path produces a different SHA-256 hash prefix for the data directory:
- bb5e6b7f8db3 (lifecycle-worker via ~/.openclaw/)
- 5128cfae53d7 (orchestrator via ~/projects_reference/)

Sessions in one namespace are invisible to the other. The lifecycle-worker polls bb5e6b7f8db3 and sees 0 active sessions, while 5128cfae53d7 has active sessions (ao-261, ao-263) that nobody is polling.

### 2. No respawn for killed sessions with open PRs
When a session is killed (exited, crashed, context exhausted) and the PR still has CR CHANGES_REQUESTED, no new session is spawned. The lifecycle-worker:
- Detects the session went to 'killed' state
- Emits an exit proof
- Fires the 'agent-exited' reaction (notify only)
- Never checks if the PR still needs work

This means PRs stall permanently once their session dies. Evidence: PRs #55, #58, #60, #61 all have CI green + CR CHANGES_REQUESTED but zero active sessions for 30+ minutes.

### Root cause
The lifecycle-worker is session-centric, not PR-centric. It polls session states and reacts to transitions. It does not ask 'are there open PRs with unresolved feedback and no active session?'

### Suggested fix
1. Consolidate to a single config path (remove ~/projects_reference/agent-orchestrator/agent-orchestrator.yaml or symlink it)
2. Add a PR-centric check in pollAll(): after processing sessions, query open PRs and spawn sessions for any PR that has CR CHANGES_REQUESTED + no active session
3. Or: change agent-exited reaction to check PR state and auto-respawn if work remains
notes: Visibility: split config namespace makes lifecycle-worker blind to sessions (see roadmap/next-priority-fixes.md § Tier 2). Parent epic bd-8wm.

title: CR COMMENTED-after-APPROVED state recovery
description: When CodeRabbit posts incremental COMMENTED reviews after an APPROVED review, the APPROVED state is superseded. Current workarounds (@coderabbitai resolve, @coderabbitai approve, @coderabbitai full review) do not reliably flip state back to APPROVED. Fix: (1) avoid triggering incremental reviews on already-approved PRs, (2) implement reliable re-approval flow, (3) consider .coderabbit.yaml config changes to auto-approve when all threads resolved.

title: Add fail-closed evidence review gate for PR-lifecycle E2E claims
description:
Problem
Review conclusions can over-credit partial evidence (pipeline E2E) as full PR-lifecycle E2E, causing scope drift across sessions.

Goal
Codify a durable, fail-closed review contract so agents cannot mark PR-lifecycle claims as PASS without explicit lifecycle evidence.

Suggestions
1) Add a claim-class matrix in docs (pipeline E2E vs PR-lifecycle E2E vs merge-gate green) with required artifacts per class.
2) Add a reusable checklist template under /tmp evidence bundles: claim, required proofs, verdict, missing proofs.
3) Add CI lint/check that fails if evidence verdict says PASS while required fields for selected claim class are missing.
4) Update AGENTS guidance to require quoting the claim class before verdict and to emit INSUFFICIENT when artifacts are incomplete.
5) Add one integration test that intentionally provides partial evidence and asserts reviewer output is INSUFFICIENT for PR-lifecycle E2E.

Required proof for PR-lifecycle E2E (minimum)
- PR creation proof (full URL + timestamp + actor)
- Transition proof (checks/reviews/comments timeline)
- Mergeability/merge outcome proof
- Cleanup proof (branch/session/worktree cleanup)

Acceptance criteria
- Documented claim-class matrix committed.
- Evidence template committed and referenced from AGENTS docs.
- Automated gate fails on mismatched PASS claims.
- Negative test for partial-evidence false positive exists and passes.
- Review output format includes explicit claim class + fail-closed verdict semantics.

title: test bead
Pattern synthesizer: generate high-confidence fix strategy templates from outcomes
description: Read outcomes.jsonl, synthesize patterns.json with winning strategies per error class. Run on configurable interval (default 4h). parallel-retry reads patterns to bias strategy selection. Ref: jleechanclaw pattern_synthesizer.py

title: EPIC: Next priority fixes — send path, namespace, merge policy, evidence (2026-03)
description:
Rolling index for highest-leverage autonomy gaps (post checkout/merge logging / curl token hardening).

Human-readable rationale + ordering: roadmap/next-priority-fixes.md

Related epics: bd-ara (autonomy blockers v2), bd-y5v (green loop) — related edges in beads.

Children (parent-child unless noted): bd-tln, bd-orch2v3, bd-qhf, bd-0gb, bd-oji, bd-e4t, bd-4n8, bd-6rh, bd-u8p, bd-ara.3, bd-s4t, bd-77b, bd-7ay.

Related (graph-safe): bd-s4t.1, bd-gim.

title: Investigate high per-cycle API call count in fork lifecycle-worker
description: Code trace reveals fork and upstream make the SAME structural calls per session (fork: 6 gh invocations with GhCache, upstream: 8 without cache). The 86-call gap is environmental (ghWithRetry retries, REST fallbacks, auto-orchestrator). 7 reduction opportunities identified in roadmap/gh-api-reduction-validation.md: (1) batch GraphQL query -60-70%, (2) throttle review backlog, (3) merge getPRState into getMergeability, (4) increase cache TTL, (5) skip getMergeability when CI failing, (6) combine PR queries, (7) disable getAutomatedComments.

title: Increase GhCache TTL from 15s to 60s
description: GhCache TTL of 15s is too short for 300s poll interval. Increase to 60s so duplicate calls across sessions in the same poll cycle hit cache. Change DEFAULT_TTL_MS in gh-cache.ts. Parent: bd-8y9

title: EPIC: Autonomy blockers — spawn-to-6-green pipeline gaps
description: Meta-issue tracking 5 systemic blockers preventing full autonomy (ao spawn → PR → 6 green → merge). Root causes identified via /harness 5-Whys analysis. Sub-issues: bd-ara.1 (comment resolution via PR desc), bd-ara.2 (mergeable=UNKNOWN), bd-ara.3 (stale session accumulation), bd-ara.4 (CHANGES_REQUESTED recovery), bd-ara.5 (test failure self-heal). See roadmap/autonomy-blockers-v2.md for full analysis.

title: Workers don't clear unresolved inline comments — document in PR desc instead of GraphQL resolve
description: Workers fix code issues but never document which comments are addressed. Unresolved thread count stays >0, blocking green condition #5. Root cause: autoResolveThreads() exists in auto-resolve-threads.ts but is NEVER called from lifecycle-manager or any runtime path — it's exported but dead code. Fix: instead of burning GraphQL to resolve threads, adopt the /copilot approach — workers document resolved comments in the PR description (cheaper, auditable). Update agentRules to instruct workers to append a '## Resolved Comments' section to PR description after fixing review feedback.

title: mergeable=UNKNOWN on 13/19 PRs — workers don't rebase stale branches
description: GitHub reports mergeable=UNKNOWN when the merge commit hasn't been computed (stale branch, no recent push). Workers never proactively rebase onto main to trigger re-evaluation. Root cause: merge-conflicts reaction only fires when mergeability is explicitly CONFLICTING, not UNKNOWN. agentRules tell workers to rebase only on conflicts, not on staleness. Fix: add agentRules instruction for workers to 'git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main && git push --force-with-lease' when mergeable=UNKNOWN, or add a lifecycle event for mergeable-unknown that triggers rebase.

title: Stale sessions accumulate past spawn gate (25 active, gate=15)
description: Sessions for merged/closed PRs stay alive because: (1) lifecycle-manager transitions status to 'merged' but never sends kill signal to tmux runtime (bd-s4t), (2) reaper skips terminal-status sessions (merged is terminal), (3) reaper only runs nightly with maxKillsPerRun=5 — too slow for 25+ sessions. Root cause: disconnect between session status tracking and tmux process management. Fix: (a) lifecycle-manager must call runtime.kill() when transitioning to merged/closed, (b) reaper should also kill sessions whose PR is merged/closed even if status wasn't updated, (c) increase maxKillsPerRun or run reaper more frequently.

title: CHANGES_REQUESTED PRs don't recover — workers fix code but don't flip review state
description: 8/19 PRs stuck in CHANGES_REQUESTED. Workers receive the changes-requested reaction and fix code, but the review state doesn't flip because: (1) workers push fixes but don't always post '@coderabbitai all good?' to trigger re-review, (2) some workers fix the wrong thing (tangential cleanup instead of the specific CR comment), (3) CR CHANGES_REQUESTED protocol in agentRules is buried in a wall of text — workers may hit context limits before parsing it. Root cause: agentRules are ~200 lines long, critical instructions compete for attention with boilerplate. Fix: (a) extract CR resolution protocol into a dedicated /copilot-style slash command that workers invoke, (b) add a post-push hook that auto-posts '@coderabbitai all good?', (c) shorten agentRules to essential directives only.

title: Test failures not self-healed — 3 PRs stuck with failing Test check
description: PRs #99, #79, #67 have failing Test checks. Workers receive ci-failed reaction but don't successfully fix the tests. Root cause: (1) ci-failed reaction message says 'Fix the failing checks' but doesn't include the actual test failure output — workers must re-derive from scratch, (2) workers often fix the wrong test or add new tests instead of fixing the existing failure, (3) no feedback loop — worker pushes a 'fix', CI fails again on the same test, worker tries a different tangent. Fix: (a) enhance ci-failed reaction context to include test failure output (not just check names), (b) add agentRules instruction: 'when fixing test failures, run the exact failing test locally first before pushing', (c) add a retry budget — after 3 failed fix attempts, escalate instead of retrying.

title: Batch GraphQL query for all per-session PR checks
description: Replace 6+ separate gh CLI invocations per session with a single batched GraphQL query that fetches state, CI, reviews, mergeable status for all PRs at once. Estimated 60-70% GraphQL reduction. Requires new SCM method (getBatchPRStatus) and lifecycle-manager restructuring. Parent: bd-8y9

title: PR poller plugin: implement PR-scanning logic that spawns sessions for non-green PRs (respawn cap 10/12h)
description: poller-manager.ts framework exists but no plugin scans open PRs. Need a poller plugin that: queries GitHub for open PRs, checks CI/review state, spawns sessions for non-green PRs with no active worktree, enforces respawn cap (max 10 per PR per 12h). Ref: jleechanclaw ao-pr-poller.sh

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: PR #129 implements backfillAllPRs in lifecycle-worker. Replaces orchestrator-session-based spawning with deterministic per-cycle spawn. In CI review.

title: Webhook ingress: HTTP server with HMAC validation and SQLite dedup
description: GitHub App webhook receiver with X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC validation, SQLite event dedup by X-GitHub-Delivery header, normalized event queue for downstream processing. Replaces need for external webhook relay. Ref: jleechanclaw webhook_ingress.py + webhook_daemon.py + webhook_queue.py

title: fix: merge-conflicts reaction periodic retry
description: merge-conflicts reaction fires once on transition then never retries. The else branch (no-transition path) has retry loops for mergeable (approved-and-green, 5min cooldown) and stuck (agent-stuck, threshold cooldown) but nothing for merge_conflicts. If agent fails to rebase, session stays in merge_conflicts forever with no further nudges. Fix: add merge_conflicts retry block in else branch with conflictRetryTimestamps map, matching the stuck retry pattern at lines 1146-1183 of lifecycle-manager.ts.

title: lifecycle-worker namespace mismatch: CWD-derived hash causes worker to read wrong data dir when started from non-canonical directory. Fix: launchd plist WorkingDirectory corrected to ~/.openclaw
fix(lifecycle-worker): 30s polling interval risks GitHub secondary rate limits (PR #120)
description: Polling interval changed from 300s to 30s (10x increase). Each poll cycle makes multiple GitHub API calls per session. With 20 sessions at 30s intervals, this risks hitting secondary rate limits (abuse detection). Batch queries help but dont fully offset. Fix: use adaptive interval (60-90s default, increase when rate limit headroom is low).

title: GraphQL-to-REST fallback for review thread resolution
description: resolveReviewThread requires GraphQL. When GraphQL rate limit is exhausted, there is no fallback. Implement: (1) detect GraphQL rate limit before attempting mutations, (2) queue thread resolutions for retry after reset, (3) explore REST-based workarounds (reply-to-comment as alternative signal).

title: GitHub API rate limit mitigation and CR state management
description: Epic for reducing GitHub API consumption during polish loops and fixing CodeRabbit review state issues. Children: bd-q3x (batch queries), bd-fy7 (GraphQL fallback), bd-77b (CR state recovery).

title: UserPromptSubmit hook: add state-guard, idempotent trigger, and exponential backoff to PR green-check reminder
description: The UserPromptSubmit hook fires the PR green-check reminder on every idle prompt, burning GitHub API rate-limit budget even when: (1) PR is already merged/closed, (2) CR review state hasn't changed since last check, (3) a known blocker (e.g. CR rate-limited) is pending. Fix: add pr-green-check.py hook that (1) skips entirely if PR is MERGED/CLOSED, (2) tracks last CR review state and skips if unchanged, (3) implements exponential backoff with jitter between checks, (4) resets backoff when CR review state transitions. Files: ~/.claude/hooks/pr-green-check.py (new), ~/.claude/settings.json (hook registration).

title: ao send pastes into dead claude sessions — review-check fix prompts never execute
description: Workers complete their initial task and the claude CLI exits. When review-check or ao send pastes a fix prompt, it hits a bash prompt, not claude. 70% of workers go idle. Superseded by bd-tln for the centralized fix.
notes: Superseded by bd-tln (canonical dead-agent spec). Keep open until bd-tln lands.

title: ao send must detect dead agent CLI and restart before pasting — all runtimes
description: P0 root cause: ao send pastes into tmux sessions where the agent CLI has exited. Fix must be centralized in tmux.ts or session-manager.ts, not per-agent. Add getRestartCommand() to agent plugin interface. Detect bash prompt vs agent prompt before sending. If dead, restart agent in existing worktree then send message. Works for claude, codex, cursor, gemini. Partially overlaps with upstream ComposioHQ#373 (bd-qhf) which fixes Enter timing but not dead-agent detection.

title: Permission bypass mode overridden by PreToolUse hooks

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Root cause: PreToolUse hooks operate independently of permission mode. Hook deny decisions block tools regardless of bypassPermissions. Jeffrey saw prompts despite bypass mode - likely edge cases in allow-list pattern matching for complex commands.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Research agent confirmed two permission layers: Layer 1 (LLM bypass mode) suppresses native prompts. Layer 2 (hooks) emits permissionDecision independently. Hook deny is by-design non-bypassable. Three hooks emit blocks: metadata-updater.sh (gh pr merge), pre_creation_blocker.sh (file creation), protect-worktrees.sh (worktree remove). Feature flag tengu_disable_bypass_permissions_mode=false confirms bypass is enabled. Verdict: hooks blocking is WAI, not a bug. If Jeffrey saw non-hook prompts despite bypass, that would be a separate issue.

title: feat: lifecycle-worker tmux session TTL / orphan sweep
Session reaper: nightly cleanup of orphaned/stale tmux sessions
description: Scheduled job (daily 3am): poll tmux for agent sessions, query GitHub for branch PR state, kill sessions that are orphaned >2h or have no PR >4h. Max 5 kills per run. Ref: jleechanclaw ao-session-reaper.sh + session_reaper.py

title: FailureBudgetTracker runtime: track retry attempts and route to escalation actions
description: ReactionConfig has failureBudget/onBudgetExhausted types (bd-uxs.3) but lifecycle-manager does not track budgets at runtime. Implement FailureBudgetTracker alongside existing reactionTrackers: count attempts per session, detect budget exhaustion, route to kill-and-respawn/notify/parallel-retry. Ref: jleechanclaw escalation_router.py

title: PR poller: batch-fetch open PRs via single REST call per repo
description: Replace per-PR gh pr view (GraphQL) with one gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?state=open (REST) call per repo. Goes from ~21 GraphQL calls/cycle to 0 GraphQL + 2 REST calls/cycle. Parse the JSON response locally to get PR number, head SHA, CI status.

title: Outcome recorder: persist winning/losing fix strategies per error class
description: Config schema for learning exists (bd-uxs.5) but no runtime code. Implement: on parallel-retry completion, append outcome to outcomes.jsonl (session ID, error class, strategy, success/fail). Query by error class to find historical win rates. Ref: jleechanclaw outcome_recorder.py

title: Merge-gate enforcement: implement 6-condition checker behind merge-gate reaction
description: merge-gate reaction type exists in config schema (bd-uxs.8) but no enforcement logic. Implement the 6 checks: CI green, mergeable, CodeRabbit approved, Bugbot clean, inline comments resolved, evidence review pass. Gate blocks auto-merge until all pass. Ref: jleechanclaw merge_gate.py [2026-03-21] PRs #59 and #61 open in jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator both implement this fix — #59 (ao-261 session) and #61 (re-dispatch). CI green on #59, awaiting CR.

title: fix(scm-github): batch query reviewDecision normalization — empty string vs null may bypass merge gate
description: Batch GraphQL query maps null/undefined reviewDecision to REVIEW_REQUIRED (pending). But empty string from GitHub may normalize differently. In lifecycle-manager, reviewDecision===none skips to approved check. If batch maps empty to none instead of pending, merge gate could be bypassed on PRs requiring reviews. Verify normalization paths match between batch and individual query.

title: harness: AO should resolve config path canonically (not CWD-dependent) so lifecycle-worker always uses correct namespace regardless of where it's started from
description: Root cause: AO uses CWD walk-up to find config, then hashes dirname(configPath) for namespace. A rogue config at ~/.agent-orchestrator/agent-orchestrator.yaml was found by workers started from inside the data dir, producing wrong namespace 923af5a19f2b. Fix: (1) deleted rogue config, (2) set launchd plist WorkingDirectory to ~/.openclaw, (3) should also set AO_CONFIG_PATH env var in plist for determinism — the env var override already exists in findConfigFile() at config.ts:433.

title: fix(send-to-agent): tmux send-keys missing Enter after message paste — agent never starts triage
description: When send-to-agent action delivers a message to an existing Claude Code session via tmux, the message is pasted into the input buffer but Enter is never sent. Agent sees message at ❯ prompt but never responds. Fix: append newline/Enter after message payload in send-keys call. Observed 2026-03-22: ao-411 through ao-420 (8 sessions) required manual Enter. Linked: orch-2v3 (jleechanclaw).

title: EPIC: Implement jleechanclaw orchestration runtime logic in AO
fix(scm-github): encodeURIComponent breaks REST branch deletion for branches with /
description: In mergePR REST fallback (scm-github/src/index.ts ~line 1485), encodeURIComponent(pr.branch) encodes / as %2F but GitHub refs API expects literal slashes. Branch deletion silently fails for most branches. Fix: remove encodeURIComponent or apply .replace(/%2F/g, '/') after encoding.

title: Harness: agents must run build+lint+test before every push
Batch PR status checking to reduce GitHub API rate limit consumption
description: Polish/fix loops make 5-15 gh api calls per PR per round. With 12 PRs and parallel agents, this burns 200+ GraphQL calls per cycle, exhausting the 5000/hr limit. Fix: (1) batch GraphQL queries to fetch all PR data in one call using aliases, (2) add TTL cache so recently-checked green PRs skip re-check, (3) prefer REST API (separate 5000/hr pool) for simple lookups, (4) reduce per-agent polling frequency.

title: EPIC: Fix GitHub GraphQL API rate limit exhaustion
description: GraphQL budget (5000/hr) is fully exhausted. PR poller wastes ~252 calls/hr on capped PRs, 15 agent sessions add ~1500+. REST budget (5000/hr) is barely used. Fix by switching to REST and eliminating wasted calls.

title: cherry-pick upstream ComposioHQ#373 send-message adaptive delay + Enter retry
description:
Upstream ComposioHQ/agent-orchestrator has fixed the same Enter-swallowed-after-paste bug we hit.

UPSTREAM REFS:
- Issue: https://github.com/ComposioHQ/agent-orchestrator/issues/373
- PR: https://github.com/ComposioHQ/agent-orchestrator/pull/541
- Branch: fix/issue-373-send-message-retry
- Design doc: docs/design-issue-373-send-message-retry.md

WHAT IT FIXES:
- Long messages (>200 chars) use tmux paste-buffer path
- Fixed 1000ms delay before Enter is insufficient for large messages
- Enter keystroke gets swallowed, agent sits idle with pasted content visible

UPSTREAM FIX (2 parts):
1. Adaptive delay: scale paste-to-Enter delay based on message length (base 1000ms + 200ms/KB, cap 2000ms)
2. Enter retry with output confirmation: after sending Enter, capture pane output and verify agent started processing; retry Enter up to N times if not

FILES CHANGED:
- packages/core/src/tmux.ts (+63 lines)
- packages/plugins/runtime-tmux/src/index.ts (+54 lines)
- packages/core/src/__tests__/tmux.test.ts (+70 lines)

ACTION: Cherry-pick into jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator. This does NOT fix the dead-agent case (bd-tln) but fixes the Enter-timing case.

title: MCP agent mail integration for inter-agent guidance messaging
description: Bidirectional messaging between orchestrator and agent sessions via MCP agent mail. Send guidance (fix strategy, context) to running agents. Receive completion/status updates. Ref: jleechanclaw mcp_mail.py + dispatch_task.py

title: /claw routing gap: main agent defaults to jleechanclaw repo, ignores target repo
description: When /claw dispatches via x-openclaw-agent-id: main, the main agent workspace (~/.openclaw/workspace) has AGENT_BASE_RULES.md wired to project_key=jleechanclaw. All tasks default to jleechanorg/jleechanclaw. Tasks intended for other repos (e.g. agent-orchestrator) silently land in the wrong repo. Observed: disable-auto-merge PR landed in jleechanorg/jleechanclaw PR #326 instead of agent-orchestrator. Workaround: always prefix task with explicit repo. Fix: create dedicated ao agent in openclaw.json with workspace pinned to ~/project_agento/agent-orchestrator, dispatch with x-openclaw-agent-id: ao.

title: Create dedicated ao agent entry in openclaw.json for agent-orchestrator tasks
description: Add agents.list entry with id=ao, workspace=~/project_agento/agent-orchestrator, agentDir pointing to ao-specific CLAUDE.md context. Dispatch with x-openclaw-agent-id: ao. Alternatively implement /clawao skill that prepends repo and path context to task prompt before dispatching to main agent.

title: Close stale open PRs from bd-uxs agent spawns
description: 13 open PRs in agent-orchestrator and 8 in jleechanclaw are all at 10/10 respawn cap. Most are stale branches from bd-uxs work. Close them to remove from poll loop. Saves API calls and reduces noise.

title: Add agent-orchestrator as AO-managed project (Option B autonomy)
description:
Add jleechanorg/agent-orchestrator as a project in ~/.openclaw/agent-orchestrator.yaml so AO lifecycle-worker autonomously drives its PRs to green+merged.

DEFERRED: AO workers consume GH API calls. With jleechanclaw already running 3 sessions, adding agent-orchestrator risks rate limit pressure. Revisit when graphql headroom confirmed >500 sustained.

ACTIONS:
1. Add project entry to ~/.openclaw/agent-orchestrator.yaml (path + repo)
2. Restart lifecycle-worker for agent-orchestrator namespace
3. Verify spawn gate (graphql.remaining > 200) enforced per poll cycle
4. Monitor first 24h API usage — baseline vs jleechanclaw consumption

title: Sessions not killed after PR merge — zombie sessions keep running indefinitely
description: When a PR is merged, lifecycle-manager.ts transitions session status to "merged" and records an outcome, but NEVER sends a kill signal to the tmux session. The runtime (tmux) process keeps running forever. Root cause: statusToEventType maps "merged" → "merge.ready" which fires the approved-and-green reaction (notify), but no code path calls runtime.kill() or sessionManager.kill() on merge. The session-reaper (bd-ls5, PR #27) is a nightly cleanup but only handles orphaned/idle/no-PR conditions — not status=merged. Fix: when a session transitions to status=merged, immediately kill the tmux session. See sessions ao-130, ao-131, ao-262 running hours after their PRs merged.

title: Session reaper: add merged-status kill condition
description: Extend reapStaleSessions() in session-reaper.ts to kill sessions with status=merged (not just orphaned/idle). A merged session has no more work to do. Alternatively, add a post-transition hook in lifecycle-manager.ts: when newStatus === merged, call runtime.kill(session). The latter is more immediate (no waiting for nightly reaper). Also update the session state file to reflect killed status after merge.

title: Agent confusion: session assigned to already-merged PR re-opens work
description: Sessions ao-131 and ao-262 were assigned to PRs #51 and #54 respectively (both merged). Because they were never killed, they kept running and evaluating whether those PRs are green — concluding they are ready to merge (they are, they already did). Two sub-issues: (1) session should detect its own PR is merged and self-exit; (2) agentRules should include explicit instruction: if gh pr view shows state=MERGED, exit immediately. Agents currently only check mergeable/reviews, not the merged state itself.

title: Combine getPRState + getReviewDecision into single gh pr view
description: determineStatus calls getPRState (gh pr view --json state) and getReviewDecision (gh api graphql) separately. Combine into single gh pr view --json state,reviewDecision to halve the calls. Parent: bd-8y9

title: fix(hooks): pr-green-check.py is a no-op that burns 2 REST calls per prompt
description: The pr-green-check.py hook always exits 0 and outputs to stderr only. Burns 2 REST API calls per prompt without suppressing the green-check. Either make it output decision JSON to stdout to actually block, or remove it. Also: branch-name PR regex is overly broad.

title: Slack notification plugin with outbox queue and dead letter handling
description: Notification plugin for Slack: task start/completion DMs, thread replies, outbox queue (JSONL, atomic writes), dead letter queue for failed sends, 30s timeout with fallback. Ref: jleechanclaw openclaw_notifier.py

title: ao send must detect dead agent CLI and restart it before pasting — all runtimes not just claude
description:
ROOT CAUSE: ao send pastes messages into tmux sessions where the agent CLI (claude, codex, cursor, gemini) has exited. The paste lands on a bash prompt and is never processed.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR:
1. Worker finishes task → agent CLI exits → tmux pane shows bash $ prompt
2. review-check or ao send pastes fix prompt into the pane
3. Text hits bash prompt, not agent CLI → message lost
4. 70% of workers go permanently idle

REQUIRED FIX (centralized in tmux.ts or session-manager.ts — NOT per-agent):
1. Before sendKeys(), detect whether the pane is at a bash prompt vs agent CLI prompt
   - Capture pane output, check for bash prompt indicators ($, bash-X.X$) vs agent indicators (❯, >, claude/codex/cursor prompt)
   - Or: check if agent process is alive via pgrep in the pane's TTY
2. If agent CLI has exited:
   a. Look up the agent plugin for this session (claude-code, codex, cursor, gemini)
   b. Restart the agent CLI in the existing tmux pane + existing worktree (no new spawn needed)
   c. Wait for agent to be ready
   d. Then send the message
3. This must work for ALL agent plugins — the restart command comes from the agent plugin interface, not hardcoded to claude

AGENT PLUGIN INTERFACE ADDITION:
- Add getRestartCommand(session): string to the agent plugin interface
- claude-code: 'claude --resume <session-id> --dangerously-skip-permissions'
- codex: 'codex --resume ...'  
- cursor: 'cursor-agent ...'
- gemini: 'gemini ...'

WHY CENTRALIZED:
- The liveness check belongs in the runtime layer (tmux.ts sendKeys or session-manager sendToSession)
- It must work regardless of which agent plugin is running
- Per-agent hacks would duplicate logic and miss edge cases

SUPERSEDES: bd-orch2v3, bd-5zr (both are symptoms of this root cause)

title: Parallel retry: add CI monitoring, winner detection, and loser cleanup
description: case parallel-retry spawns N sessions (bd-uxs.4) but nobody monitors which succeeds. Need: poll CI status across spawned sessions, detect first-green-wins, kill losing sessions, promote winner branch. Ref: jleechanclaw parallel_retry.py

title: Agents calling gh pr merge directly — bypassing orchestrator merge gate
description: AO sessions (jleechan2015 token) merged PRs #51, #53, #54 autonomously by running gh pr merge inside the tmux session. This bypasses the lifecycle manager entirely — no merge gate check, no notification, no outcome recording. The agentRules for the agent-orchestrator project do NOT include an explicit NEVER MERGE instruction (unlike jleechanclaw config which does). Agents are deciding to merge on their own. PRs #51, #53, #54 merged seconds apart (automated). Fix: add explicit NEVER run gh pr merge or any merge command to agentRules in agent-orchestrator.yaml. The orchestrator merges after all 6 gate conditions pass.
notes: Roadmap index: roadmap/next-priority-fixes.md · epic bd-8wm (2026-03-24). metadata-updater hook + AO_ALLOW_GH_PR_MERGE mitigate direct merges but do not replace merge-gate enforcement in product/config.

title: Add NEVER MERGE instruction to agentRules in agent-orchestrator.yaml
description: Add to the agentRules block in ~/.agent-orchestrator/agent-orchestrator.yaml: NEVER run gh pr merge or any equivalent merge command. Your job ends when the PR is green (all 6 conditions met). The orchestrator handles merging. Post a PR comment "PR is green X/6 — awaiting orchestrator merge" then stop. This mirrors the working jleechanclaw config pattern.
close_reason: done — added NEVER MERGE + exit-on-merged rules to agent-orchestrator project agentRules in ~/.openclaw/agent-orchestrator.yaml

title: EPIC: Absorb jleechanclaw orchestration patterns into AO core
Fix workspace dirty bug: AO self-dirties worktrees on ~/.openclaw layout
PR poller plugin: scan open PRs, spawn sessions (outer initiation loop AO is missing)
Escalation router: policy-driven failure budgets + deterministic routing in lifecycle-manager
Parallel retry action: speculative parallel CI fix sessions, first-green-wins
Outcome recorder + pattern synthesizer: closed-loop learning from CI fix outcomes
Session exit reconciliation: detect exits, validate commits pushed, emit proof payload
Agent sessions: use REST API for PR status checks
description: Agent sessions (12 jcc-* + 3 cao-*) call gh pr view/checks which burns GraphQL. Add agentRules guidance to use gh api REST endpoints instead. Or patch ao agent-base to use REST for PR checks.

title: Duplicate PRs when re-dispatching: no dedup for existing open PR on same task
description: When a task is re-dispatched (/claw re-run), AO spawns a new session with no awareness that an existing open PR already covers the same task. Result: PRs #59 and #61 both implement the merge-gate fix (same code, two PRs). PRs #60 and the jleechanclaw PR #326 both implement disable-auto-merge. Fix: before spawning, query open PRs for branches matching the task scope. If one exists, send the agent to that existing PR instead of creating a new one. Or: lifecycle manager should detect duplicate branch names and redirect.

title: Pre-spawn dedup check: query open PRs before creating new session for same task
description: In the spawn path (session-manager or lifecycle-manager), before creating a new session: (1) query open PRs for the project, (2) check if any branch name or PR title matches the task keywords, (3) if match found, assign the new session to that existing PR instead of starting fresh. Alternatively, expose a spawn --reuse-existing flag that agents can use when re-dispatching failed tasks.

title: Skip getMergeability when CI is failing
description: In determineStatus(), if getCISummary returns 'failing', skip the getMergeability call (saves 3 gh CLI invocations per session). Currently getMergeability is called even when CI is red — pointless since the PR can't merge anyway. Parent: bd-8y9

title: harness: add 2x-recurrence escalation trigger to CLAUDE.md
feat: spawn-time stale worktree recovery (git exit 128 on ao spawn)
Auto-resolve stale PR review threads after fix push
description: SUPERSEDED by bd-ara.1 — instead of GraphQL thread resolution, workers document resolved comments in the PR description (/copilot approach). The auto-resolve-threads.ts code exists but is dead code (never called from lifecycle). Original: When a fix commit is pushed, auto-resolve GitHub review threads whose file location was modified. Uses GraphQL to query unresolved threads, filters bot-only threads. Helps unblock PRs stuck in UNSTABLE merge state. Ref: jleechanclaw auto_resolve_threads.py

title: EPIC: Green loop — shepherd PRs from current state to 6-green autonomously
description: End-to-end validation of the autonomy pipeline. Instead of building more plumbing, run a recurring loop that picks the nearest-to-green PR, does the next action (rebase, fix CR comments, trigger re-review, document resolved comments), and repeats until at least one PR reaches 6-green. Success = one PR merged by AO without human intervention. Depends on bd-ara (blockers identified). This epic proves the pipeline works.

title: Green loop v1: recurring 10m loop to shepherd nearest-to-green PR to merge
description: Implement a /loop 10m task that: (1) lists open PRs sorted by proximity to 6-green, (2) picks the top candidate, (3) executes the single highest-leverage action (rebase for UNKNOWN mergeable, post @coderabbitai for stuck CR, document resolved comments in PR desc, fix failing test locally), (4) reports what was done and what remains. Each iteration should do ONE action and move on — not try to fix everything at once. The loop validates that the autonomy pipeline works end-to-end. Success criteria: at least one PR reaches 6-green and is merged by the orchestrator auto-merge reaction.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: PR #129 adds backfill spawning + reaction observability. Next: verify E2E green loop.

title: feat: reaction observability logging in lifecycle-worker
description: Added lifecycle.reaction.start/result/skipped/send_failed observer entries to executeReaction() and transition handler. PR #129.

title: Throttle maybeDispatchReviewBacklog to every Nth poll
description: maybeDispatchReviewBacklog calls getPendingComments + getAutomatedComments every poll cycle even when nothing changed. Add a poll counter to only run review backlog every Nth cycle (e.g. every 3rd). Saves 2+ calls/session on skip cycles. Parent: bd-8y9

title: PR poller: check respawn cap BEFORE making API call
description: ao-pr-poller.sh fetches PR info via gh pr view (GraphQL) before checking if the respawn cap is hit. All 21 open PRs are at 10/10 cap — 252 wasted GraphQL calls/hour. Fix: read the local respawn counter first, skip the API call entirely if cap is already reached.
